title: Cross-Environment Multi-Paradigm Motor Imagery EEG Dataset
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 84 healthy participants performing motor imagery tasks across multiple controlled environments (laboratory and simulated hospital), with a supplementary cohort of 3 participants recorded in microgravity conditions in a space station environment. The dataset includes multiple motor imagery paradigms (Graz, SSMVEP-MI, and hybrid variants) with 64-channel EEG recordings at 1000 Hz sampling rate (69 channels acquired, 60 channels used in analysis), designed to investigate environmental effects on brain-computer interface performance and enable cross-environment robustness evaluation. This dataset is a republication of OpenNeuro ds007221 on the NEMAR platform to enhance accessibility and discoverability within the neuroimaging research community.
methods_description: EEG data were collected using a 64-channel system following the 10-20 electrode placement system (69 channels acquired, 60 channels used in analysis) at 1000 Hz sampling rate in the main dataset. Motor imagery tasks included left-hand, right-hand, feet, and rest conditions organized in trials with cue periods (−2 to 0 s), task periods (0 to 4 s), and rest periods (4 s). Recordings were conducted in two distinct environments in the main dataset: electromagnetically shielded laboratory and simulated hospital with multi-sensory elements. The supplementary space station dataset employed an EGGO system with 32 dry electrodes and was recorded in microgravity conditions.
